This is it and what was funny is I Started off with a headline and all it has is just a headline a call to action and a place to enter the their email address That's all that's up here and it started out as a fairly long form offer and what I found is when I remove some of the bullet points conversions went up Remove a couple of the other bullet points conversions went up remove some of the other copy conversions went up So this means I either really suck as a copywriter or you have to match The offer that you're making with the kind of how high is the buried entry if you're just asking for an email address In some cases it doesn't make sense To have a really long drawn-out process some cases it does it all depends again on traffic source But right now still what's worked the best? It's not true You just have to do it the right way We now have a through through through the agency that we work with who runs a lot of our cam our Google campaigns We're able to talk They're able to talk straight with the people at Google and we're actually able to get a lot of our pages Pre-approved ahead of time before we put them up there So we're like hey guys instead of us putting up our page and you slapping us and I was getting mad at you and I was trying to Just blindly fix it tell us before we even go what we need to do and if you spend enough money and you work with a good agency They can get that service done for you and but what we hear time and time again from from Google is That they hate Hypiness the owners of Google Larry and Sergey specifically said that they want to rid of the internet of infomercial type marketing Yousers and In the pack what you want to say is like what's great that works. There's nothing wrong with it. It's been around forever But for these guys They still got a huge chunk of the pie guys We got to play by their rules if you want to go there. It's tough. You can't talk anything about making money. All right It's just learned about real estate. I mean, it's got to be the most tepid You got to like throw out everything you thought you knew about good copywriting for to work in Google and by that I mean real estate investing not if you're Selling stuff to real estate agents, but even then you can't say hey You know, here's here's how to make you know million dollars as real estate agent I had a Google page slapped in an account band For a hot dog cart offer We were showing people how they could make money Running a hot dog cart. Come on I mean you call yourselves an American company and you're gonna crap on the hot dog guy You can't do that. So they don't care if you're talking about money making you're gonna have to tweak it a little bit But so for the page itself, this is a landing page right now that we're doing that's working This is built on a WordPress platform. Okay, it's built on a WordPress platform And this violates pretty much every single rule that you ever thought you knew about generating leads online Number one Google wants to see a logo Logos matter logos are a big trust factor and how many you guys have heard all you don't want to have logos You just want to have a blind opt-in form anybody heard that Guess I'm the only one. Thanks mom If you've heard that in the past that was true We tested having logos or having headers versus not having them and it converted today With the trust factor being what it is on the internet everybody being a little bit scared and worried We're actually getting increased conversions by having logos Professional logos again if it looks funky, it's gonna suppress response Not like there's anything magical about a logo if you just type something out and like you know times new Roman and you know cut it out And paste it up and it doesn't match the background and looks all funky You're gonna look all funky and they're not gonna trust you so professional logos matter Not just from a conversion standpoint in some cases, but definitely to Google. They also want to see navigation This was the biggest kick in the you know what of all because we now We've all been told that if you have a web page that has navigation on it's going to suppress response, haven't we? We've been told that we actually did a test during our last forex launch and We drove we split tested the affiliate traffic drove half of it to a blog that had navigation and half of it to a standard squeeze page Much like the one I showed you before The opt-in rate was I think 48% to the one with the blog and 49% to the one Not on the blog it didn't seem to matter all that much and we're finding that more and more and more Navigation is not hurting conversion rates still don't necessarily recommend it on your non-google traffic, but on your Google traffic It's not the death knell. We once thought it was This is a biggie a non-hypey offer the original lead magnet for this for this particular offer was basically how to survive the coming You know apocalypse and you know there's like a mushroom cloud in the background and all this stuff and they were like no Can't use fear-based advertising in Google anymore They don't want to see it. They got no interest in fear-based advertising. They want happy positive fun-loving crap. That doesn't work But that again, that's that's that's their reality So we we dulled it down it hurt conversion rates But here's the good thing that you guys need to understand about Google all right because I don't want to be all glum Make you think like oh man none of this stuff works a good friend of mine Well, we're out of it He made one of the best analogies that I've ever heard in my entire life and I'm gonna take all the credit for it right now But what he said was Google what it's been over the past few years. It's been much of crack dealers It's at we're in the hood and it's everybody slinging crack rock Okay, and so if you've got a legitimate offer if you don't want to be ultra-hypey If you're selling snow cones for example, you're screwed. You don't have a chance You can't sell snow cones next to a guy selling crack everybody wants crack right so Especially when they're given the first one way for free. Well, what Google has effectively done is they have rid Their space of crack dealers So now if you want to sell snow cones, that's cool You can do it because you're not having to sell against Crack dealers so we've seen a lot of our prices in a lot of markets go down on a cost per click standpoint because we don't have the same number of competitors and In general, we just don't have to we don't have to hype stuff up as much. It's really nice Google wants the additional articles and content. This also sucks. This does suppress response But you got to have it you got to have it if you notice though it is all below the fold Okay, we got the offer up top. It's below the fold. They do want to see relevant images, but not scary images One of the images that a guy in our office had it literally had a little girl sitting in a wheat field like like kind of rocking her baby doll and in the back there's like this, you know There's like a nuclear silo that's about to explode. We're like, you know what that might be a little much And Google agreed they asked us to replace that image We also though and you see it down there at the bottom We do have a below the fold opt-in if people scroll to the bottom give an opportunity to take action We don't have quite the same iPad. Okay, I wish we could get quite the same iPad There's an interruption with the text, but that's what they want to see And here's a biggie Here's a big big biggie one of the number one reasons that people get their account slapped and they don't know it because Google won't tell you because they're jerk faces is If you don't have a privacy policy and the terms of service clearly visible on your landing page, they will slap you How many of you guys knew that? Yeah, it's a big deal We couldn't figure out for the longest time what we were doing wrong for the longest time what we're doing wrong and finally It's like we just forgot to put the terms of service in a privacy policy statement on there You have to have it It is in their terms of service that you have that it is a violation of terms of service to be driving traffic to a Google landing page That doesn't have terms of service and privacy policy if you're gathering information So that's how those are kind of some basic basic things to keep in mind for the Google friendly Let's talk about reverse squeeze pages real quick. Why do you think that is? Because they hadn't watched this one all the way It's a consumption issue when they have to consume your content To then take action. There's a problem. Okay in some cases a lot of your affiliates will not send straight to a Traditional landing page like the two I just showed you before they're gonna want to send people to content They're gonna want to send people to content and they're gonna ask that you build them a reverse reverse squeeze page Because again the content is given on the front end then the actions taken then they can get more content What we would rather do we tell affiliates who are like hey, we don't want to send traffic straight to that We'll tell them that don't worry about it. I'm a big believer in what's the first transaction that you have with somebody So if your initial transaction is kind of not so hot if people don't have the best feeling about it It's probably gonna come back to bite you later on So I really want when somebody goes through that thing for that first transaction for them to be thinking this was great This person really over delivered and what somebody opposite you send to a sales page That's not so great in terms of a first transaction All right But if you test it, I mean kind of depending on your you can kind of make a moral or an ethical call there But if you test it a lot of times this will this will out pull other things because lead magnets can sometimes Derail the buying process and we'll talk about that a little bit more So this is an example people opt-in and then they get seen a sale sent to a sales letter as long as your sales letter does have some content by the way, which most of them do It's I don't really have a big problem with this it can be it's like anything else You know you can use it for the forces of good or you can use it for the forces of evil Let me go back and check Sign up for your free report now. Yeah, so they're promising a free report of sorts. There's your free report have fun reading that sales letter now But I mean how often I mean how I mean look I bought the a-to-z info publishing I mean, this is nothing new. This is what's been done in the offline world forever You you publish an ad you say, you know send in a thing to request your free report and what do you send it out a Sales letter. It's just the online version of that So again, I don't have any moral or ethical issues with it Certainly as long as your sales that are delivers some content which the typically the best sales messages do but You know you kind of got it got a play around with that The way that Motley fool does it is actually a lot better when people opt-in you go to fool.com and opt into some of their stuff If you want to see their process They have people opt-in and then they actually send them to a report which is essentially an article on different pages And then like the fourth page begins sales process It's a version of this of the sandwich page, but I'm gonna show you guys a version that's a lot better a lot more profitable in just a second So here's here's the the name squeeze page. I showed you guys this but we actually do promise a lead magnet We actually do send them a report So it's into your email address in the form below and I'll send you free instant access to Forex gap trading the forgotten trading system That is right 88.1 percent of the time Now in this case what we did is we sent them the report via email So we did actually fulfill on the promise, but as soon as they opted in they went to a page that said You know the you know check your email inbox in a couple of minutes You know down the link for the report's gonna be there while you wait You know read this special report or watch a special video and that was when we showed on the sales message Again that works. Well, you guys could test that in a second. I'm gonna show you a version that works even better Reports versus videos reports versus videos Right now in most of the markets that we're in and again, this is more a function of market maturation than it is This is a broad broad rule, but in most of the markets that we're in offering a PDF report is Getting a higher opt-in rate than offering a video Video has been devalued. It's been overdone. There's YouTube people getting videos sent to him all the time It's not the novelty that it once was in some markets. It still is okay So you got to test this stuff what we do is we give them both and the example that I showed you before People got a report and they got a video so we can also hit the multiple modalities, but I talked before Offering videos in this case didn't work this worked better, but the thing that worked the best was offering a report Thing that worked the best was offering a downloadable PDF report what worked even better than that Hand-drawn reports hand-drawn reports and I got this idea from Frank Kern How many you guys saw his anybody on his mass control launch when he did the finger report? It's just he just drew a picture of a middle finger because it's because that's he's frank and that's what he does But that was his highest like Generated way more leads at anything else that he did because it's got that personal interaction, you know I mean if we all admire Bill and Dan But you know if they type something out and send it to us, that's one thing if you get their hand written Bill it's a much higher perceived value. Why do people collect autographs? How many of you guys were here when Cal Ripken? You know was here and you were getting him to autograph stuff. What do you do that? That's bizarre you can just go online and print out a picture that's got his name on it You know what his name is anyway You don't need to be reminded by some squiggly lines It's because he wrote it with his own hand and that still has a lot of meaning for us in this in our culture So when you go through the trouble of doing that or having somebody else go through the trouble of doing that for you If you have zero artistic ability like myself That's gonna result in some of the highest opt-in rates that you can get either way having a flow chart or a mind map Something that simplifies Whatever it is that you're teaching them make people go gaga. Why cuz we are lazy We are so lazy. I don't want to read a 50 page report I want a single sheet that's gonna like oh go to this point this point this point It's gonna be very systematic Gonna tell me exactly how I need to do it. So I don't have to exercise any effort or work whatsoever that's what people want and flow charts and mind maps and Checklists cheat sheets you saw the offer that we had before it was the Forex trading cheat sheets All those things work really really really well people are totally dig that stuff And we tested across a number of different markets Here's a biggie that you may not have thought of and I talked about it a little bit when I was up on the panel but offering what we call self-liquidating offers in exchange for leads so What's you know, what would you rather have a lead that gave you some money or an opt-in? Lee that gave you some money, right? I've heard it stated. I don't know, you know, it's one of those things you can keep quoting Statistics and as long as they're old enough and you don't know the original source. They must be true But I've heard it said enough times from enough good sources that I believe it somebody that gives you any money at all Even if it's just a buck is 10 to 20 times more valuable than somebody who just pops in That's the principle behind this self-liquidating offer and this is something that we saw some years ago And it's this guy that sells He sells Guitar strings For classical guitars, so not the metal ones the I don't play the guitar. What are what are the guitar strings that are not metal called? nylon thank you He sells nylon guitar strings and he basically sells them at cost Okay, he basically sells them at cost and my buddy who who's a big classical Taurus. He's like man. I love this guy You know, he's such an idiot. There's no way he's making money on these guitar strings. I know they cost a fortune Well, what the guy's doing is he's direct importing guitar strings And he's literally selling them at the same cost that he's buying them from the factory now Why would this guy do this big dumb idiot, right? Why would he do this? Well, my friend bought some guitar strings from him a week later He got an email sent to him to hey you want to buy I got some guitars for sale 1200 bucks What do you think he did? Wrong he bought two But what is he what is this person what is this eBay idiot doing who's out there selling stuff and not making any money? He's generating the most valuable list of all a buyer's list a buyer's list and he's doing it for free It's not cost him anything. What'd you say bud? Yeah, and he's yeah, he's creating a value perception so It's a great and he doesn't just have to be on eBay It doesn't just have to be on eBay at all sometimes We'll go out to our list and we'll sell something very very low ticket just to reactivate people just to get him to take out Their dang credit cards, you know, it's it's a magical thing that happens The momentum that's generated when somebody takes out their credit card There's so much more likely to keep it out than if it always stays in their wallet So think self liquidating offers think what can offer that's a physical item Even if I don't make any money at all That I can give away and let people keep you want to go really nuts If you got an affiliate program let your affiliates do something like this and keep all the money They get to sell a physical product and keep all the money You're building a buyer's list you're building a buyer's list so think these one of the most profitably met and almost Nobody does it almost nobody in your market do this you can create much more valuable offers than anybody else in your market Okay, so the big lead magnet paradox Not having a lead magnet can hurt your opt-in rates It can also affect that initial first transaction that you have with your people right so the way to get around this whole lead Magnet issue is to do what I talked about in the beginning not have one people opt-in. Thanks. Here's your sales letter But if you don't want to do that right if you want to have a really good first transaction If you want to deliver tremendous value you need to have a lead magnet, but having lead Mac it can derail the sales process Somebody go opt-in They get your free report They you know they go to read it. They think all this is great. They don't finish it. They put it down They tell themselves. I'm gonna get to that later when is later never Later equals never put that in your brain anybody's who's been ever been in retail or Car sales has probably heard of the phrase be backs Be backs how many be backs you get today? I had you know nine people come in. They're really interested They're gonna be back tomorrow. No be backs. They're air vapor nothing. They're worth this Okay, and you know when you offer something That is of really good value and people kind of get into it, but they don't finish it There are be back. So here's some ways that you can mitigate that number one Don't stretch out the delivery process of your content How many you guys currently have when people opt-in a seven-day mini course on blah blah blah blah? Okay, stop doing that. Okay, you're It's okay to have a seven-day course if they opt-in for day one and you you fulfill day one And then you let them go ahead and go on to day two what I used to do was people would opt-in and We'd send them day one they'd email in to customer support Which was me at the time and but I was I was replying as like Sally or something because I didn't want people knowing I was doing my own customer support So, you know, I replied to them. They'd be like can I get day two? I'm really excited about this. No, no, no You have to wait I'm building anticipation Boy, they're gonna be so stoked to buy by the time. No Day two they were kind of stoked day three Get a little disinterested day four. Who is this person day five? You're a spammer Okay, so you can have a multi-part e-class, but let them cheat Okay, if they want to go through all seven days in one day, let them do that Nothing wrong with that at the end of one say to watch next to click here. Boom. There's one. Let them walk through all of them Okay, if they're hot Nobody will be more excited about what it is that you have to offer They will never be more excited about what it is that you have to offer it Then they were that moment when they first opted in that was when they were the most excited You don't know what happened You don't know what entered into their life at that particular moment You know, maybe they got the letter in the mail that their high school reunion is coming up and they're not feeling You know, they want to lose some weight before that happened You know, maybe they got the news that that there's gonna be a layoff at their office You don't know maybe if you wait seven days, they might find out good news. You're not laid off and they're like screw it Don't need this stuff. Okay. They the moment that they found you they sought you out. They found you Give them at least the opportunity to give you money then Don't force them to wait or you will lose out on a lot now the people that aren't ready. Yes You want to have follow-ups you want to nurture them, but for the people who were hot ready to go Give them the option to give you money and you'll find that they give you more money. It's a crazy process All right, so So not dragging out the buying process of the other big thing that you can do in your lead magnets If you want to mitigate this issue is don't have these massive You know massive big lead man. I know people you opt in it's like get the free report It's like a 280 page report. I don't need to buy anything from you right now. I got to get through this thing first Okay, stop doing that crap Show value you don't have to show value in terms of bulk Show value in terms of quality here The so the answer to this whole thing is what we call the sandwich page because basically what you're doing is you are sandwiching a Small sliver of content between the opt-in and the sales process It creates a nice slick shoot to go through and the example I'm going to show you which pains me to show it because I don't like to give this guy credit because no, he's a Buddy of mine, but he's he's arrogant. Yes, Evan pagan. That was so genius, you know what you did here He was like I just was bored and didn't want to write more, but it worked out great So here's this here's a squeeze page a great short form squeeze page people opt-in The promise is you're about to learn secrets that most men will never know about women and Inside you'll learn the kiss test how to tell if She's ready to be kissed The difference between how men and women think about dating and why blah blah blah, but the big bullet point the kiss test Everybody who opts in Wanted to learn the kiss test How can I know if she's ready to be kissed and he knew that because he pulled his people that was one of their big concern for Men on a first date, you know, it's that awkward moment like I've you know Is she ready? He knew that was a big hot button So he put that right up on top and when you opt in what do you get you get the kiss test here? It is this is the magical kiss test. It's a whopping one two three four five six paragraphs two of them are only one line That's the kiss test. How long would it take you to read that? Go ahead read it time it I got no not long not long maybe a minute no time at all But what's cool about it is you instantly get value you read it and you say huh? I bet that works. I want to try that So value is given it's about quality not quantity Tremendous value is given, but it's so easily consumed look you can dump a library on people But if they never consume it, they're never going to experience the value all right give them an opportunity to experience the value by giving them an opportunity to consume it So come up with whatever your coolest best idea is and give them that and the thing is that's been said How many you guys have heard that come up with your best thing and give it away? Right this concept called the free line you guys heard that ebb and ebb and talks about it It is one of the most un misunderstood Concepts in marketing that I've ever heard in my life because people say oh my best stuff Well, I'm gonna give them a whole freaking course because that's my best stuff. No, they're not gonna consume it guys Give them your best thing. What's the really really cool trick that you can tell them about that they go? This guy knows his stuff. I can try I go out tonight. I can try that and experience value That's what free line content is all about so be thinking about that Don't be thinking what's some special report if right now if you're giving away a Seven-part course on something probably somebody told you you need to have a seven-part course Probably only had one idea good idea and the rest of them kind of filler ditch the other ones okay? It's not about quantity It's about quality. Okay part two We're gonna talk about getting your emails opened getting your emails open is a function of one thing and one thing only Well, that's a function of two. The first thing. It's a function of is do they know you? All right, I'm assuming that you're not spamming people. Okay, so setting that aside. Hopefully you have some relationship Hopefully they know you But the main thing that getting your emails open is a function of is the subject line So here's some proven subject lines You want to think in terms of odd numbers having weird odd numbers in your subject lines? Why you paid Google 524 thousand eight hundred thirty eight dollars and seventy one cents odd numbers They don't necessarily have to be money numbers, but odd numbers are good question marks Question marks in you know you ask a question. There's an assumption that there's going to be an answer on the inside Minted the question mark Percentages similar to question marks. They're kind of a weird little digit 99% of people dieting need to do this Having the little bracketized video new video or a free report or putting PDF You guys seen that putting a if you're if you're sending somebody to download a free PDF test putting PDF in brackets in your subject line And are E as a reply now. This is Overdone a lot and people will put in re when it's not actually a reply. Let me tell you the right way to do an re To do a reply in a subject line that is also means less work for you So it's sort of you know double double duty if you send out an email promotion and You want to do an update to it or something like that you send out an email and then you just do a reply Copy and paste you know the previous email that you sent below and then write another message It just says hey, I realize I forgot to mention this in my previous email below So I wanted to make a special point of it or hey, I don't know if you got my email below But the offer ends on Thursday, please read it then you're essentially replying to your email that you sent out So you do re the same subject line you had before It's a really good strategy deliver some heavy open rates if you do it every day You just look like a tool so don't do that and there are some people that every freaking email I get from says re I And you know personal pronouns you your etc Some other proven subject lines peak curiosity confuse or shock Here's a good one that that you'll love Bill and I don't know if Dan's in the room, but though you're saying one of the one of the best Oh like one of the good ways that you all tested for for envelopes Teasers on envelopes was do not bend photos enclosed. I just love the phrase chuckle head. Don't take it personally, but Oh, I hate technology that was one that I sent out and one word dot dot dot crazy All those things kind of peak curiosity get get the open rate So there's just those some subject lines you can you can kind of play with and again driving traffic calm forward slash Ryan Handouts dot PDF you can have these for your own swipe files time your email delivery is also helps to function Is a function of open rate best time we mail out at 430 8 m central standard time 430 a.m. Central Standard Time that pretty much ensures because when do most people check email first thing in the morning I pretty much ensures that we're right there Best days Tuesday through Thursday in a big tip Weekends are great for low dollar and lead generation offers a lot of people think oh don't mail on weekends Nobody's checking their email horse crap. You guys check your email on weekends. You just don't have time to necessarily read them all So take advantage of the vacuum in most market places by mailing on the weekends when your competitors are not But don't mail high ticket offers mail something they can look at reading God. It's cool. Bye Part three getting the click-through We've kind of quickly here short emails as a general rule get higher click-through rates And I believe that the job of the email is to get the click. I'm not necessarily trying to sell an email I like to get the click so the shorter the email if your email comes in and most people's browser And it's all above the fold They could see it all right there They'll typically make a decision to go ahead and read it if they got a scroll Then they'll make a decision of do I want to read this now or do I want to wait and read it later when it's later? Never so you know unless it's unless you just got a really great reason try to keep it all about the fold Highest click-through rate link placement have a link right after the first paragraph Have a link midway down in the body and have a link in the close of the ps Every email that we send out I don't say every email because I'm like you said email yesterday I don't have one pretty much every email that we sent out certainly if I'm the one that's writing it has three links in it Right after the first paragraph Again pick off pick the low-hanging fruit get the people who need to know more and then get the people who dropped right down to the ps And read that it's the same three types of markets Images and emails increase click-through rates up to a hundred and twenty percent Here's what I mean if you're sending people to watch a video Take a screenshot of the video Insert it you got to do an HTML email do this but insert it into your actual email where the play button is visible If you see a play if you see what looks like a video in your email inbox people click on it go cool I can watch it. Oh, oh, is it a new video in a new window? But you still got to click and as long as the video that starts playing is the same video that they thought they clicked on There's nothing wrong with it You know you're delivering what what the promise is but if it's just an image The click-through rates aren't near as good as if it's an image a screenshot of the video with the play button still visible Okay, the play button is what makes that work I got I Tell you what I'm gonna do I got we're now moving to sneaky tricks which we're gonna talk about after lunch so I will go ahead.
